column1: # TLC model-checking manifest — the coverage ledger (the TLC analogue of the
# Verus trusted-base ledger). scripts/tla-baseline.sh reads it for both its
# model list and its coverage-regression assertion targets.
# distinct-states is the coverage metric: a drop means TLC explored a smaller
# behaviour set, i.e. the model now proves *less*. diameter is the worker-invariant
# structural depth; it must not change for an equivalent model. Both are
# independent of -workers and -fp.
# Re-derive the expected numbers from a real local run — never trust a stale
# table. `-` means "not yet pinned": tla-baseline.sh records the observed value
# and prints it but does not assert. Replace `-` with the run's number to arm
# the regression guard.
# Canonical CONSTANTS (kept here so the lock-step cfgs cannot silently drift):
#   cap_revocation revoke set  (Spec @ CapRevocation.cfg / SpecBad / SpecNoGuard
#     — the liveness arm and its two negative controls, must match in lock-step):
#     CapIds={c0,c1,c2,c3} Procs={p0,p1} Channels={ch0} Threads={t0}
#     Notifs={nf0,nf1} QueueDepth=1 NULL=NULL
#     This liveness arm checks only TypeOK + the EventuallyRevoked liveness
#     property; the safety invariants (MoveSemantics/DeadNowhere/LiveParent/
#     FireSafe/RevokedDead) and the ReportMonotone action property are subsumed by
#     the CapRevocation_Safety arm below (a strict superset of states), so they are
#     not re-evaluated on this heap-bound tableau. distinct/diameter are graph
#     metrics, independent of which invariants are checked, so these pins are
#     unchanged by that trim.
#   cap_revocation fairness control  (SpecExistFair @ CapRevocation_NegFairness.cfg):
#     the revoke set above but CapIds={c0,c1,c2,c3,c4} — one cap above the floor.
#     SpecExistFair reuses Spec's Init/Next and weakens the per-cap WF on RevokeStep
#     to a single existential WF; the starvation lasso it must exhibit (one revoking
#     subtree churned forever while another persists) needs two disjoint revoking
#     subtrees under a non-revoking root, which is unreachable below five caps — the
#     4-cap floor HOLDS even under the existential WF. Intentionally one cap above
#     the floor, so deliberately NOT in the lock-step set above. A liveness control
#     (no SYMMETRY); like the other negative controls it is not a pinned row — TLC
#     stops at the first lasso, so it has no full distinct/diameter to assert.
#   cap_revocation safety arm  (Spec @ CapRevocation_Safety.cfg): as above but
#     Threads={t0,t1} QueueDepth=2 — restores the two residence axes the liveness
#     arm trims to fit its tableau. Intentionally LARGER (added safety coverage),
#     so deliberately NOT in the floor lock-step set above. Carries SYMMETRY
#     SafetySymmetry; its pinned distinct is the post-quotient orbit count (the
#     full reachable set is 12,183,480, quotiented to 635,034 by collapsing each
#     Procs x CapIds x Threads permutation-orbit — group order 96; adding the
#     size-2 Threads axis is a near-ideal 1.95x on top of the Procs x CapIds
#     quotient — same behaviours, fewer representatives).
#   cap_revocation teardown    (TSpec): as above but Threads={t0,t1} QueueDepth=2;
#     carries SYMMETRY NotifSymmetry — pinned distinct is the nf0<->nf1 orbit
#     count (252 full reachable, quotiented to 132).
#   commit_protocol            (Spec / SpecNeg / SpecAsymBad): Refs={r1,r2,r3}
#     MaxWrites=2 NULL=NULL — Spec and both negative controls carry SYMMETRY
#     RefSymmetry; the pinned distinct is the post-quotient orbit count
#     (2,480,585 full reachable, quotiented to 413,455 by collapsing each Refs
#     permutation-orbit — group order 6, a near-ideal 6.0x reduction).
#   ipc_reactor                (Spec + 3 controls): MaxMsgs=3 QueueDepth=2
# Columns (tab-separated): name  spec_relpath  cfg  expected_distinct  expected_diameter
name
CapRevocation
CapRevocation_Safety
CapRevocation_Teardown
CommitProtocol
IpcReactor
